model_narrative: We plan a forestry and forest-industry operation. We decide how much new forest of each species to manage on each quality of land and at what age to harvest it, how many logs of each type to supply to industry, how hard to run each conversion process, how much mill capacity to install, and how much final product to ship. Running the operation earns sales revenue and incurs investment, processing, cutting, and planting costs. The objective is to maximize the total discounted net benefit, that is revenue less all of those costs.

sets.name: c
sets.doc: all commodities flowing through the system, covering both raw log types harvested from the forest and the final manufactured products

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the final products that are shipped and sold; a subset of the commodities

sets.name: cl
sets.doc: the log types harvested from the forest and supplied to industry; a subset of the commodities

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the tree species that can be planted and grown

sets.name: k
sets.doc: the site classes describing land quality

sets.name: at
sets.doc: the tree age brackets at harvest, in ten-year steps from 10 to 80 years

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the industrial processes that convert logs into products

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the productive units (mills) whose capacity is sized to the processes they run

params.name: scd
params.doc: the fraction of each species' land that falls in each site class; the shares sum to one across site classes

params.name: land
params.doc: the land available for each species, in thousands of hectares

params.name: ymf
params.doc: the yield of each log type from managed forest, in cubic metres per hectare, depending on tree age at harvest, site class, and species

params.name: a
params.doc: the input-output coefficient of each process for each commodity; negative entries consume the commodity as input, positive entries produce it as output

params.name: b
params.doc: how much of each productive unit's capacity a unit of each process consumes

params.name: pc
params.doc: the operating cost of each process, in US dollars per cubic metre of input

params.name: pd
params.doc: the sales price of each final product, in US dollars per unit

params.name: nu
params.doc: the investment cost of each productive unit, in US dollars per cubic metre of input capacity

params.name: age
params.doc: the age in years associated with each age bracket, equal to ten times the bracket position

params.name: mup
params.doc: the planting cost, in US dollars per hectare

params.name: muc
params.doc: the cutting cost, in US dollars per cubic metre of logs harvested

params.name: life
params.doc: the plant life in years, used to annualize investment

params.name: rho
params.doc: the discount rate per year, used to annualize investment and to compound planting outlays forward over the growing period

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the area of new forest of each species managed on each site class and harvested at each age bracket, in thousands of hectares per year

vars.name: r
vars.doc: the supply of logs to industry for each log commodity, in thousands of cubic metres per year

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the activity level of each process, in thousands of cubic metres of input per year

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the installed capacity of each productive unit, in thousands of cubic metres of input per year

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the final shipments of each commodity, in thousands of units per year; only the final products are actually shipped

vars.name: phik
vars.doc: the annualized investment cost, in thousands of US dollars per year

vars.name: phir
vars.doc: the total process operating cost, in thousands of US dollars per year

vars.name: phix
vars.doc: the total sales revenue, in thousands of US dollars per year

vars.name: phil
vars.doc: the total cutting cost, in thousands of US dollars per year

vars.name: phip
vars.doc: the total planting cost compounded forward to harvest, in thousands of US dollars per year

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted net benefit, equal to revenue minus all cost components

constraints.name: r[cl]
constraints.intent: The logs supplied to industry come entirely from the new forest that is grown and harvested. For each log type, the quantity supplied to industry must equal the total harvested over every species, site class, and harvest age, where the amount harvested from each combination is its area managed multiplied by the per-hectare yield of that log type for that species, site class, and age.

constraints.name: a[c,
constraints.intent: Every commodity must balance, meaning supply cannot fall short of what is used or shipped. For each commodity, the net amount produced across all processes, plus any logs supplied to industry when the commodity is a log type, must be at least the amount shipped as a final product when the commodity is a final product, and otherwise at least zero.

constraints.name: b[m,
constraints.intent: Each mill must have enough capacity installed to run the processes assigned to it. For each productive unit, the total capacity used by the processes, weighted by how much of that unit each process consumes, must equal the capacity installed at that unit.

constraints.name: v[s,
constraints.intent: Planting is limited by how much land of each quality is available for each species. For each species and site class, the area managed across all harvest ages, with each area weighted by its harvest age in years, must not exceed the land available for that species multiplied by that site class's share of the land.

constraints.name: phik
constraints.intent: The annualized investment cost reflects the cost of installing mill capacity spread evenly over the life of the plant at the given discount rate. Set the investment cost equal to the annualizing factor times the total installed capacity valued at each unit's per-unit investment cost.

constraints.name: phir
constraints.intent: The total process operating cost adds up the cost of running every process. Set the process cost equal to the sum over all processes of each process's activity level times its per-unit operating cost.

constraints.name: phix
constraints.intent: The total sales revenue adds up what is earned from shipping the final products. Set the sales revenue equal to the sum over all final products of the amount shipped times its sales price.

constraints.name: phil
constraints.intent: The total cutting cost depends on how many logs are harvested. Set the cutting cost equal to the per-cubic-metre cutting cost times the total volume of all log types supplied to industry.

constraints.name: phip
constraints.intent: The total planting cost values each planted area at the planting cost and compounds it forward to harvest at the discount rate over the growing period. Set the planting cost equal to the per-hectare planting cost times the sum over every species, site class, and harvest age of the area managed, with each area compounded forward by the discount rate over its number of growing years.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: The total discounted net benefit is the revenue left after paying for everything. Set the net benefit equal to the sales revenue minus the investment cost, the process cost, the cutting cost, and the planting cost.

query: We're looking at a regional build-out target that the two mills together have to clear: total installed throughput capacity across the pulp mill and the saw mill should come in at no less than 900 thousand m3/year so the whole processing complex is sized for the projected log flow. With that combined capacity floor in place, where does the total benefit land?
expected_pyomo: model.total_capacity_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.h[m] for m in model.m) >= 900.0)

query: There's a proposal on the table to keep the saw mill from being squeezed out entirely: the saw-mill line should hold at least a tenth of the total installed capacity across both mills, where the total is the pulp-mill capacity plus the saw-mill capacity. If we put that balance rule on the build, what does the optimizer return for total benefit?

query: We're evaluating a product-coverage requirement for the regional market: every one of our final products — both pulp and sawnwood — has to ship at least 40 thousand units a year so each line stays commercially viable. Apply that uniform shipment floor across the final products and tell me where total benefit ends up.
expected_pyomo: model.product_coverage_floor = Constraint(model.cf, rule=lambda m, c: m.x[c] >= 40.0)

query: There's a two-part commissioning package under review for the processing complex. First, the build has to stand up at least 850 thousand m3/year of total installed capacity across both mills so the complex is sized for the projected harvest. Second, the saw mill itself has to be commissioned at a minimum viable scale of at least 60 thousand m3/year of throughput rather than being left as a stub. Run the package and tell me what the plan looks like.
expected_pyomo:
model.complex_capacity_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.h[m] for m in model.m) >= 850.0)
model.sawmill_min_scale = Constraint(expr=model.h['saw-mill'] >= 60.0)
